## 🔌 Wiring Table

| Arduino Pin | Component | Function |
|-------------|-----------|----------|
| **D2** | Display – segment **A** | Top bar |
| **D3** | Display – segment **B** | Top-right bar |
| **D4** | Display – segment **C** | Bottom-right bar |
| **D5** | Display – segment **D** | Bottom bar |
| **D6** | Display – segment **E** | Bottom-left bar |
| **D7** | Display – segment **F** | Top-left bar |
| **D8** | Display – segment **G** | Middle bar |
| **GND** | Display – GND | Common cathode |
| **D10** | Button – pin A | Input (with internal pull-up) |
| **GND** | Button – pin B | Button ground |

---

## 💻 How the Sketch Works

- **`showDigit(n)`** — maps 0–9 to the correct 7 segments using an if/else chain (no 2D arrays)
- **`INPUT_PULLUP`** — no external resistor needed for the button; pin reads HIGH at rest, LOW when pressed
- **Debounce** — 200ms delay after each press prevents multiple counts from one press
- **Wrap-around** — after 9, counter resets to 0

---

## ⚡ Power Budget

| Item | Current Draw |
|------|-------------|
| Each lit segment (~5V, virtual) | ~10 mA |
| Max segments ON at once (digit 8) | ~70 mA |
| Button (pull-up, negligible) | < 1 mA |
| **Total max draw** | **~71 mA** |

> ✅ **Safe** — Arduino Uno can source up to 200 mA total from its 5V rail, and each I/O pin stays well under its 40 mA limit (one segment per pin ≈ 10 mA each).

---

### 🎮 How to use
Press the **button** to count up: `0 → 1 → 2 → ... → 9 → 0`. The display updates immediately on each button press!
